data.title: Whale Tips: Best Ways to Onboard & Train Teams Faster
data.excerpt: Discover top Whale tips for SOPs and team training. Cut onboarding time in half with AI-powered documentation. Start free today!

$media.alt: Whale Tips: Best Ways to Onboard & Train Teams Faster

children.text: *Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you purchase through them, I may earn a small commission at no extra cost to you.*

I've spent years testing productivity tools, and every once in a while one genuinely shifts how a team operates. Whale is one of those tools. If you've ever watched a new hire drown in scattered Google Docs, outdated Notion pages, and "just ask Sarah" conversations, you already know the problem Whale solves.

It's a platform built specifically for documenting standard operating procedures, creating internal training courses, and getting people up to speed without the chaos. And the AI features? They turn what used to be a weekend project into something you can knock out during a Tuesday afternoon.

I'm going to walk you through exactly how to get the most out of Whale — tips, best practices, and the features that make it worth your time. Whether you're a startup founder wearing twelve hats or an HR lead at a mid-size company, there's something here for you.
**Try Whale free and see the difference for yourself**
What Exactly Is Whale and Why Does It Matter?
Whale is an
AI-powered SOP platform

that gives your team one central place to document, share, and manage every process that keeps your business running. Think of it as the brain of your operations — except it doesn't forget things when someone goes on vacation.

Here's the core idea: instead of burying critical knowledge in email threads, Slack messages, or that one Google Doc nobody can find, you put it all inside Whale. Processes get documented once, updated easily, and shared with the right people at the right time.
The platform covers three big pillars:
SOP Documentation
— Write, organize, and maintain standard operating procedures with AI assistance that actually speeds things up.
Employee Onboarding
— Build structured onboarding paths so new hires hit the ground running instead of hitting walls.
Ongoing Training
— Assign courses, track completion, and keep skills sharp across your entire organization.
What I appreciate most is how Whale doesn't try to be everything. It's not a project manager. It's not a chat app. It does one thing — knowledge transfer — and it does it exceptionally well.
New and Highlighted Features You'll Love
Whale has been rolling out some genuinely impressive updates. If you tried it a year ago and moved on, it's worth a second look. Here are the standouts:
AI-Powered SOP Generation

This is the headline feature, and it delivers. You can start with a rough bullet-point description of a process, and Whale's AI will flesh it out into a clean, structured SOP. Step-by-step instructions, formatting, even suggested visuals. It doesn't replace your expertise — it accelerates it. I tested this with a client onboarding workflow and had a polished document in under ten minutes.
Smart Templates

Whale offers a growing library of templates for common business processes — think sales workflows, customer support scripts, HR procedures, and IT protocols. You're not starting from scratch. Pick a template, customize it to your team's specifics, and publish. The templates are genuinely useful, not just placeholder text.
Video and Screen Recording

Sometimes a written SOP isn't enough. Whale lets you embed video walkthroughs and screen recordings directly into your procedures. For complex software steps or hands-on tasks, this is a game-changer. Your team sees exactly what to do, not just read about it.
Quizzes and Knowledge Checks

After someone completes a training module, you can attach a quiz to verify they actually absorbed the material. It sounds simple, but this one feature closes the gap between "I read it" and "I understand it." Completion rates go up, mistakes go down.
Integration Ecosystem

Whale plays nicely with the tools you already use. Slack notifications for new assignments, browser extensions for quick access, and integrations with platforms like PartnerStack for affiliate and partner teams. You're not adding another silo — you're connecting the dots.
Want a deeper dive into every update? Check out
this breakdown of Whale's new features
Who Is Whale Ideal For?
Not every tool fits every team. Here's where Whale shines brightest:
Fast-Growing Startups

When you're hiring two or three people a month, you can't afford to have each onboarding be a one-off event. Whale lets you systematize the process so growth doesn't break your operations. One founder I spoke with cut onboarding time from three weeks to ten days after implementing Whale.
Remote and Distributed Teams

If your team spans time zones, you can't rely on someone being available to walk a new hire through a process in real-time. Whale documents become the always-available guide. No waiting, no bottlenecks.
Agencies and Service Businesses

Agencies juggle multiple clients, each with different processes. Whale makes it easy to create client-specific SOPs and ensure every team member delivers consistent quality regardless of which account they're working on.
HR and People Operations Teams
This one's obvious, but worth stating. If onboarding and training are part of your job description, Whale was essentially built for you. The tracking dashboards alone save hours of follow-up work.
Franchise and Multi-Location Businesses
Consistency across locations is the whole game. Whale ensures every store, office, or branch follows the same procedures — and updates propagate everywhere instantly.
For a full exploration of how different industries use the platform,
here's a guide to Whale use cases
7 Tips and Best Ways to Use Whale Like a Pro
Alright, let's get tactical. Here are the practices that separate okay Whale users from power users:
1. Start with Your Most Painful Process

Don't try to document everything at once. Pick the process that causes the most confusion or the most repeated questions. Maybe it's client onboarding, maybe it's how to process a refund, maybe it's the sales demo script. Document that one thing thoroughly, publish it, and watch the immediate relief on your team's faces. Then move to the next one.
2. Use the AI, Then Add Your Voice

Whale's AI generates a solid first draft, but the magic happens when you layer in your team's specific language, examples, and quirks. Maybe your sales team calls a certain step "the handshake moment" or your support team has a specific tone they use with customers. Add those details. The AI handles structure; you add soul.
3. Assign Ownership to Every SOP

Every document in Whale should have an owner — someone responsible for keeping it current. Outdated SOPs are worse than no SOPs because they create false confidence. Whale makes ownership assignment easy, and it sends reminders when documents haven't been reviewed in a while. Use this religiously.
4. Build Onboarding Paths, Not Just Documents

A pile of SOPs isn't an onboarding program. Whale lets you create sequenced learning paths where new hires go through documents and training modules in a specific order. Day one: company overview and tools setup. Week one: core role procedures. Month one: advanced processes. Map it out once, reuse it forever.
5. Record Your Screen for Software Workflows

If your process involves clicking through a software tool — a CRM, an admin panel, a design app — record it. The screen recording feature in Whale means you don't have to write "click the dropdown in the upper right corner, then select 'Settings,' then navigate to the 'Billing' tab." You just show it. Three-minute video replaces a wall of text that nobody reads carefully.
6. Add Quizzes to Critical Training Modules

For processes where mistakes are costly — compliance procedures, financial operations, safety protocols — attach a quiz. It takes two minutes to set up and gives you documented proof that your team understood the material. This is also clutch for audits and regulatory compliance.
7. Review and Refresh Quarterly

Set a recurring calendar reminder. Every quarter, audit your SOP library. Are there documents nobody has viewed in months? Archive them. Are there processes that changed but the documentation didn't? Update them. Whale's analytics show you exactly which documents are being used and which are collecting dust.
Need a step-by-step walkthrough of the entire setup?
This Whale how-to guide
covers everything from account creation to advanced features.
Getting Started with Whale: My Honest Recommendation
Here's what I'd do if I were you, right now:
Step 1:
Sign up for the free plan. Whale's free tier includes 5 members and 1 creator, plus full AI access. That's enough to run a real test with your team.
Step 2:
Pick one process and document it using the AI generator. Spend 15 minutes refining it.
Step 3:
Share it with your team and ask for feedback. Did it clarify things? Did they find gaps? Iterate.
Step 4:
If it clicks (and it will), upgrade and start building out your full SOP library and onboarding paths.

The difference between teams that struggle with knowledge transfer and teams that scale smoothly often comes down to one tool. Whale is that tool for documentation and training. It's not trying to replace your project manager or your chat app. It fills a specific, critical gap that most companies leave wide open.
I've seen teams go from "where do I find that process?" to "check Whale" in a matter of weeks. That shift is worth more than any feature list.
**Start your free Whale account today and build the onboarding system your team deserves**

children.text: Is Whale really free to start?
Yes. Whale offers a Free Forever plan that includes 5 members, 1 creator, and full AI features. It's enough to test the platform with a small team before committing to a paid plan.
How does Whale's AI SOP generation work?

You provide a rough outline or bullet-point description of a process, and Whale's AI expands it into a structured, formatted SOP with clear steps. You can then edit, add visuals or screen recordings, and publish. It turns hours of writing into minutes.
Can Whale replace tools like Notion or Google Docs for internal documentation?

Whale is purpose-built for SOPs and training, whereas Notion and Google Docs are general-purpose tools. Whale includes features they lack: AI-assisted SOP creation, onboarding paths, quizzes, completion tracking, and document ownership reminders. If standard operating procedures and team training are your focus, Whale is the sharper tool.
What integrations does Whale support?

Whale integrates with tools like Slack for notifications, offers a browser extension for quick SOP access, and connects with platforms like PartnerStack. The integration ecosystem continues to expand, so check their site for the latest list.
